---
title: "Run CoPro on D1 and D2 neuron data"
output: html_document
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "/Users/zhenmiao/Dropbox/Zhuang-ABCA-1.054_1/")
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

## load packages 
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='hide'}

library_paths <- c('fields', 'ggplot2', 'tictoc', 'irlba', 
                   'Matrix', 'ggsci', 'ape', 'CoPro')
# Load required libraries
lapply(library_paths, library, character.only = TRUE)
```

## set some parameters 
```{r }
n_pca = 40
cell_types = c('061 STR D1 Gaba', '062 STR D2 Gaba')
n_cell_types = length(cell_types)

horizontal_dist_scale <- 1
vertical_dist_scale <- 1
```




## Load data
``` {r}
ra <- readRDS('Zhuang_ABCA_1.054_subset_data.rds')
meta <- readRDS('Zhuang_ABCA_1.054_subset_metadata.rds')
```

## subset data
```{r}
subset_cells <- meta$subclass %in% cell_types 
  
ra = ra[subset_cells,]
meta = meta[subset_cells,]
rownames(meta) <- meta$cell_label
## make sure coordinates are numeric 
meta$x = as.numeric(meta$x)
meta$y = as.numeric(meta$y)
```

## show the in situ plot 
```{r}
ggplot(data = meta)+
  geom_point(aes(x = x, y = y, color = subclass))+
  theme_minimal()+
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1)
```


## load data into object 
``` {r}
location_data <- meta[,c('x','y')]
cell_type_data <- meta[,'subclass']
br <- newCoPro(normalizedData = ra,
               locationData = location_data,
               metaData = meta, 
               cellTypes = cell_type_data
                      )
br <- subsetData(br, cellTypesOfInterest = cell_types)
```

## compute distances
```{r}
br <- computePCA(br, nPCA = n_pca, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
sigma_square_choice = c(0.001, 0.002, 0.005, 0.01, 0.02, 0.05,0.1, 0.2,0.5)
br <- computeDistance(br, distType = "Euclidean2D")
br <- computeKernelMatrix(br, sigmaSquares = sigma_square_choice)
```

## run skrCCA and obtain correlations
```{r}
br <- runSkrCCA(br,scalePCs = TRUE,maxIter = 500)
br <- computeNormalizedCorrelation(br)
```



```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
